frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: perceptual choice as accumulation of sensory evidence over time

frameworks.name: history-dependent decision bias
frameworks.one_line: prior choices/stimuli shape current decision formation

frameworks.name: adaptive prior expectations
frameworks.one_line: biases flexibly tuned to environmental correlation structure

core_question: How does the correlation structure of the sensory environment shape history biases acting on neural decision formation?
key_assumptions: perceptual choices accumulate noisy evidence over time
history biases can be inferred via trial-by-trial behavioral modeling
action-selective motor cortical activity indexes decision formation
biases reflect prior expectations rather than baseline activity shifts
manipulations: correlation structure of the stimulus sequence across blocks
trial-by-trial variation in preceding stimuli, choices, and outcomes
key_findings: history bias was flexibly adapted to environmental correlation structure
bias acted selectively on the build-up of action-selective motor cortical activity
bias did not modulate baseline level of motor cortical activity
history-bias effect added to the impact of the current stimulus
interpretations: motor-cortical action plans are shaped by dynamic, environment-tuned prior expectations
adaptive interaction with the environment biases evidence accumulation rather than starting point
